reasoning: The only difference between the two formalizations is `x ^ n` (ORIGINAL) vs `↑x ^ n` (CORRECTED) on the right-hand side of the hypothesis `h`, where `x : I = Icc 0 1` and `f n x : ℝ`. In Mathlib (`Mathlib/Algebra/Order/Interval/Set/Instances.lean`), there is an instance `instPow : Pow (Icc (0 : R) 1) ℕ` defined by `pow p n := ⟨p.1 ^ n, …⟩`, and the lemma `coe_pow : ↑(x ^ n) = ((x : R) ^ n)` holds by `rfl` (i.e., definitionally). Therefore, in the ORIGINAL, `x ^ n` elaborates via the `Pow I ℕ` instance to produce a value in `I`, which is then coerced to `ℝ` via the expected-type mechanism; this is definitionally equal to `↑x ^ n` in the CORRECTED. Both forms denote the same proposition. The other differences (`open Topology` vs `open scoped Topology`, `noncomputable section`) are purely stylistic and don't change the theorem statement. The informal statement (that the sequence `f_n(x) = x^n` converges pointwise on `[0,1]`) is a classical, unambiguous, true fact. The ORIGINAL formalization is faithful.
